messages.message: Hi can you send catalogue and price on pilates beds please

Hi , Thanks for reaching out. To help us recommend the right Pilates Reformer, could you please tell us: Are you buying for a studio, gym, or resale business? How many units are you planning to purchase? Which market/country will the machines be delivered to?
We are a gym, we would be looking at potentially close to 50 units
And for the style, do you prefer standard reformer, foldable reformer, half tower, full Cadillac, or megaformer? Once I know your preference, I can send you the most suitable catalogue options.
Hi Paul, thanks for sharing. For a gym setup with around 50 units, may I first confirm which type you’re looking for: aluminum, wooden, or stainless steel?
Hi Paul, Just following up on your gym project. For a setup around 50 units, we would usually narrow it down to 2–3 suitable commercial options first, instead of sending too many models at once.
If helpful, I can shortlist the most suitable options for a gym setup like yours and send you only the relevant catalogue pages to review.

Some gyms prefer a classic standard reformer setup, while others go for a more complete training configuration with tower options. It mainly depends on how you want to position the space and the type of classes you plan to offer.
We are looking at the standard reformer not the tower...but a high end version
Also, some gyms at your scale include a few megaformers as an additional offering — not essential, but it can help expand class variety. Is that something you’re considering as well?

Hi Paul, For high-end gym setups, most clients typically go with aluminum reformers — they’re more durable and give a cleaner, more professional look for commercial use. Would that direction work for your space? If yes, I can shortlist a few high-end models that fit your setup and send you details and videos.

last_my_message: Hi Paul, For high-end gym setups, most clients typically go with aluminum reformers — they’re more durable and give a cleaner, more professional look for commercial use. Would that direction work for your space? If yes, I can shortlist a few high-end models that fit your setup and send you details and videos.
